title: mildly fermented Middle-Eastern bean sprouts Tagine
description: Pungent, tender, light Middle-Eastern dish featuring bean sprouts, fermentation, and anti-inflammatory-friendly ingredients in a cazuela. Perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Rinse and soak the kombu seaweed in water for 4 hours.
Drain and chop the kombu seaweed into small pieces.
Heat the olive oil in a cazuela over medium heat.
Add the thinly sliced red onion and sauté until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the mixed bean sprouts, cumin, coriander, fenugreek powder, salt, and black pepper. Stir well.
Add the chopped parsley and soaked kombu seaweed pieces. Stir well.
Pour in the lemon juice and stir well.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes.
Remove from heat and let it ferment at room temperature for 2-3 hours.
Note: The dish should have a slightly tangy and earthy flavor from the kombu seaweed and fermentation.
